retinal_sim - Full Validation Audit Report

Generated: 2026-04-04 16:19

Scope of This Report

This audit report shows the implemented validation checks, the exact pass criteria used, the observed outcomes, the assumptions and limitations attached to each check, and the code paths that produced the result. Statements here are evidence-backed for the current proof-of-concept build, not blanket claims of full physiological completeness.

Summary

15/15 implemented validation checks passed under the current test harness

Known Limitations / Deferred Architecture Items

  • Visual-inspection checks remain labeled as visual/manual evidence, not strict quantitative proof.
  • Several validation checks use proof-of-concept simplifications documented in SCRATCHPAD.md.

Architecture Coverage Matrix

Architecture itemImplemented testCode pathStatusNote
Architecture §11e Angular subtense correctnessAngular Subtenseretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredAnalytic geometric equality check.
Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across speciesRetinal Scaling Across Speciesretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredChecks focal-length proportionality.
Architecture §11e Accommodation/defocus injectionAccommodation Defocusretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredHard-cutoff accommodation model.
Architecture §11e Distance-dependent receptor samplingDistance-Dependent Receptor Samplingretinal_sim/pipeline.pycoveredCounts stimulated receptors inside the projected object footprint.
Architecture §11a Metamer preservationMetamer Preservationretinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.pycoveredUses a fixed deterministic near-metamer regression dataset under D65.
Architecture §11a Roundtrip consistencyRGB Round-Tripretinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.pycoveredUses the same D65 observer model as the basis construction for reprojection.
Architecture §11a Spectral response orderingSpectral Response Panelretinal_sim/validation/report.pycoveredUses deterministic RGB probes to sanity-check cone-catch ordering under the D65-based reconstruction model.
Architecture §11b Diffraction-limited resolutionMTF vs Diffraction Limitretinal_sim/optical/psf.pycoveredMeasures empirical sinusoidal contrast transfer against the implemented PSF MTF.
Architecture §11b PSF energy conservationPSF Energy Conservationretinal_sim/optical/psf.pycoveredFloat64 normalization check.
Architecture §11c Snellen acuity predictionSnellen Acuityretinal_sim/validation/acuity.pycoveredOrdering plus published-range checks.
Architecture §11c Dichromat confusion axisDichromat Confusionretinal_sim/validation/dichromat.pycoveredUses fixed confusion/control stimulus matrices with median-based comparisons.
Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limitNyquist Samplingretinal_sim/retina/mosaic.pycoveredConverts local cone density to cycles/degree using focal-length magnification.
Architecture §11c Receptor count validationReceptor Countretinal_sim/retina/mosaic.pycoveredCompares measured 1 mm² counts against density-model expectations.
Architecture §11d Known visual deficit reproductionColor Deficit Reproductionretinal_sim/pipeline.pycoveredEnd-to-end species comparison.
Architecture §11d Resolution gradientResolution Gradientretinal_sim/output/reconstruction.pycoveredQuantifies center-vs-periphery contrast loss on a checkerboard stimulus.

Open Code Review Findings

Read this report alongside CODEREVIEW.md and the generated status report. Validation pass/fail status does not replace code review findings or architecture audit notes.

Test Details, Evidence, and Provenance

Angular Subtense PASS

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Angular subtense correctness
Expected outcome
2*arctan(w/(2d))
Observed outcome
w=0.2m, d=1.0m → 11.4212° (expected 11.4212°) ✓ w=1.0m, d=10.0m → 5.7248° (expected 5.7248°) ✓ w=0.3m, d=6.0m → 2.8642° (expected 2.8642°) ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Absolute angular error < 0.01 degrees for every case.
Inputs summary
Three analytic scene width/distance cases using the human optical model.
Method
Compare computed angular width against the closed-form geometric equation.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
Assumptions
  • Exact 2*arctan geometry is the source of truth.
  • Human optical focal length is used only to build SceneDescription.
Limitations
  • Checks representative cases, not exhaustive parameter sweeps.
Artifacts
  • Bar chart: expected vs computed angular width
w=0.2m, d=1.0m → 11.4212° (expected 11.4212°) ✓
w=1.0m, d=10.0m → 5.7248° (expected 5.7248°) ✓
w=0.3m, d=6.0m → 2.8642° (expected 2.8642°) ✓

Retinal Scaling Across Species PASS

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across species
Expected outcome
retinal_width ∝ focal_length
Observed outcome
human: retinal_w=1.1150mm, focal=22.3mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ dog: retinal_w=0.8500mm, focal=17.0mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ cat: retinal_w=0.9250mm, focal=18.5mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Species ratios agree within 1% relative error.
Inputs summary
One shared physical scene evaluated for human, dog, and cat species configs.
Method
Compare retinal_width_mm / focal_length_mm across species.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
Assumptions
  • Retinal image width should scale with focal length when angular size is fixed.
Limitations
  • Only validates proportionality, not downstream sampling behavior.
Artifacts
  • Species focal-length bar chart
  • Species retinal-width bar chart
human: retinal_w=1.1150mm, focal=22.3mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
dog: retinal_w=0.8500mm, focal=17.0mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
cat: retinal_w=0.9250mm, focal=18.5mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓

Accommodation Defocus PASS

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §0 Accommodation demand and residual defocus
Expected outcome
max(0, demand - max_accommodation)
Observed outcome
human: demand=4.0D, max_acc=10.0D, residual=0.00D (expected 0.00D) ✓ dog: demand=4.0D, max_acc=2.5D, residual=1.50D (expected 1.50D) ✓ cat: demand=4.0D, max_acc=3.0D, residual=1.00D (expected 1.00D) ✓
Tolerance
0.1
Pass criterion
Residual defocus matches expected value within 0.1 diopters.
Inputs summary
Near-view scene at 0.25 m for human, dog, and cat.
Method
Compare computed residual defocus against max(0, demand - max_accommodation).
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
Assumptions
  • Accommodation is a hard cutoff.
  • Max accommodation values are fixed PoC constants.
Limitations
  • No lead/lag accommodation response model is applied.
Artifacts
  • Expected vs computed residual defocus bars
human: demand=4.0D, max_acc=10.0D, residual=0.00D (expected 0.00D) ✓
dog: demand=4.0D, max_acc=2.5D, residual=1.50D (expected 1.50D) ✓
cat: demand=4.0D, max_acc=3.0D, residual=1.00D (expected 1.00D) ✓

Distance-Dependent Receptor Sampling PASS

Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Distance-dependent receptor sampling
Expected outcome
~1/d² scaling
Observed outcome
d=1m → 51332 stimulated receptors d=2m → 9240 stimulated receptors d=4m → 2373 stimulated receptors d=8m → 575 stimulated receptors stimulated-count ratio (d=1.0m/d=8.0m): 89.3 vs expected 64.0 (rel err 0.39) ✓
Tolerance
0.4
Pass criterion
Far/near stimulated-receptor scaling stays within 40% relative error of ideal 1/d^2.
Inputs summary
Constant-size grayscale object that remains smaller than the 2° patch across all tested distances.
Method
Measure stimulated receptor counts at multiple viewing distances and compare against ideal inverse-square scaling.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/pipeline.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
Assumptions
  • Stimulated receptors are those whose mapped positions fall inside the projected object footprint.
Limitations
  • Density gradients and stochastic mosaic sampling still introduce some deviation from exact inverse-square behaviour.
Artifacts
  • Distance vs stimulated receptor count plot
  • Log-log ideal vs measured scaling plot
d=1m → 51332 stimulated receptors
d=2m → 9240 stimulated receptors
d=4m → 2373 stimulated receptors
d=8m → 575 stimulated receptors
stimulated-count ratio (d=1.0m/d=8.0m): 89.3 vs expected 64.0 (rel err 0.39) ✓

Metamer Preservation PASS

Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Metamer preservation
Expected outcome
Max cone-catch relative difference < 3% for fixed near-metamer dataset
Observed outcome
cyan_blue_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0123 for [13, 150, 234] vs [52, 148, 235] ✓ teal_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0146 for [70, 192, 203] vs [106, 189, 203] ✓ green_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0273 for [61, 245, 158] vs [24, 249, 158] ✓ violet_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0296 for [127, 41, 209] vs [101, 60, 207] ✓
Tolerance
0.03
Pass criterion
Maximum cone-catch relative difference < 3% for every dataset pair.
Inputs summary
Fixed near-metamer RGB pair dataset integrated against human receptor sensitivities under D65.
Method
Upsample each RGB pair, integrate against human sensitivities, and bound the maximum cone-catch relative difference.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/opsin.py
Assumptions
  • Dataset is repo-local and deterministic to keep the audit reproducible.
Limitations
  • Dataset is compact rather than exhaustive; it is intended as a deterministic regression panel.
Artifacts
  • Representative pair spectra plot
  • Per-pair max cone-catch difference bars
cyan_blue_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0123 for [13, 150, 234] vs [52, 148, 235] ✓
teal_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0146 for [70, 192, 203] vs [106, 189, 203] ✓
green_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0273 for [61, 245, 158] vs [24, 249, 158] ✓
violet_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0296 for [127, 41, 209] vs [101, 60, 207] ✓

RGB Round-Trip PASS

Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Roundtrip consistency
Expected outcome
RMSE < 2.0 for all colours
Observed outcome
Max RMSE = 0.00
Tolerance
2.0
Pass criterion
RMSE < 2 8-bit counts for all tested colours.
Inputs summary
Eight canonical sRGB colors passed through spectral upsampling and D65-based reprojection.
Method
Project upsampled spectra back to sRGB and compute per-colour RMSE.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
Assumptions
  • The same D65-referenced observer model used to build the basis spectra is used for reprojection.
Limitations
  • This validates the D65 round-trip path, not arbitrary illuminant changes.
Artifacts
  • RMSE bar chart
  • Input vs reconstructed color swatches
Red: in=[255, 0, 0] out=[255,0,0] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Green: in=[0, 255, 0] out=[0,255,0] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Blue: in=[0, 0, 255] out=[0,0,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Yellow: in=[255, 255, 0] out=[255,255,0] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Cyan: in=[0, 255, 255] out=[0,255,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Magenta: in=[255, 0, 255] out=[255,0,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
White: in=[255, 255, 255] out=[255,255,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Gray: in=[128, 128, 128] out=[128,128,128] RMSE=0.00 ✓

Spectral Response Panel PASS

Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Spectral response ordering
Expected outcome
Anchor RGB probes preserve expected cone-catch dominance and blue-vs-warm separation across species
Observed outcome
human: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->M_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone human: sep(blue,yellow)=0.525, sep(blue,red)=0.583 dog: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone dog: sep(blue,yellow)=0.845, sep(blue,red)=0.793 cat: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone cat: sep(blue,yellow)=0.796, sep(blue,red)=0.799 OK: Human violet is S-dominant OK: Human blue is S-dominant OK: Human green is M-dominant OK: Human amber is L-dominant OK: Human red is L-dominant OK: Human yellow remains dominated by L+M cone catch OK: Human yellow keeps low S contribution relative to blue OK: Dog blue is S-dominant OK: Dog amber is L-dominant OK: Dog red is L-dominant OK: Dog blue-yellow separation remains substantial OK: Dog blue-red separation remains substantial OK: Cat blue is S-dominant OK: Cat amber is L-dominant OK: Cat red is L-dominant OK: Cat blue-yellow separation remains substantial OK: Cat blue-red separation remains substantial
Tolerance
0.45
Pass criterion
Anchor colors preserve expected cone-type dominance and dog/cat blue-vs-yellow/red separations exceed the fixed margin.
Inputs summary
Deterministic RGB probe panel spanning violet-to-red anchors plus mixed colors, integrated with D65-weighted species sensitivities.
Method
Upsample each probe color, compute D65-weighted cone catches for each species, and verify expected dominance/order and blue-vs-warm separations.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/opsin.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/datasets.py
Assumptions
  • The panel is RGB-defined and deterministic; it is a regression sanity check rather than a physical monochromator dataset.
Limitations
  • Because the probes originate from sRGB values, this validates response ordering under the current D65-based reconstruction model rather than true narrowband physiology.
Artifacts
  • Representative reconstructed spectra plot
  • Per-species normalized cone-catch panel
human: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->M_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone
human: sep(blue,yellow)=0.525, sep(blue,red)=0.583
dog: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone
dog: sep(blue,yellow)=0.845, sep(blue,red)=0.793
cat: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone
cat: sep(blue,yellow)=0.796, sep(blue,red)=0.799
OK: Human violet is S-dominant
OK: Human blue is S-dominant
OK: Human green is M-dominant
OK: Human amber is L-dominant
OK: Human red is L-dominant
OK: Human yellow remains dominated by L+M cone catch
OK: Human yellow keeps low S contribution relative to blue
OK: Dog blue is S-dominant
OK: Dog amber is L-dominant
OK: Dog red is L-dominant
OK: Dog blue-yellow separation remains substantial
OK: Dog blue-red separation remains substantial
OK: Cat blue is S-dominant
OK: Cat amber is L-dominant
OK: Cat red is L-dominant
OK: Cat blue-yellow separation remains substantial
OK: Cat blue-red separation remains substantial

MTF vs Diffraction Limit PASS

Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §11b Diffraction-limited resolution
Expected outcome
Measured sinusoidal contrast transfer matches predicted MTF within 0.08
Observed outcome
Max |measured-predicted| = 0.0720
Tolerance
0.08
Pass criterion
Measured modulation stays within 0.08 of the predicted MTF at all tested spatial frequencies.
Inputs summary
Human 550 nm PSF tested with sinusoidal gratings spanning low-to-mid spatial frequencies.
Method
Measure empirical contrast transfer on sinusoidal gratings and compare it with the PSF-predicted MTF.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/optical/psf.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
Assumptions
  • The implemented PSF is a diffraction-derived Gaussian approximation.
Limitations
  • Validation targets the implemented diffraction-derived PSF model, not a full Airy-pattern optical stack.
Artifacts
  • Measured vs predicted MTF plot
  • 550 nm PSF kernel image
5 cpd: measured=0.9909, predicted=0.9909, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
10 cpd: measured=0.9642, predicted=0.9642, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
20 cpd: measured=0.8659, predicted=0.8659, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
30 cpd: measured=0.8021, predicted=0.7301, |Δ|=0.0720 ✓

PSF Energy Conservation PASS

Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §11b PSF energy conservation
Expected outcome
|sum(PSF) - 1.0| < 1e-6
Observed outcome
human: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓ dog: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓ cat: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓
Tolerance
1e-06
Pass criterion
Maximum |sum - 1.0| < 1e-6.
Inputs summary
Gaussian PSF kernels across the canonical wavelength grid for all three species.
Method
Sum each PSF kernel and measure max absolute deviation from 1.0.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/optical/psf.py
Assumptions
  • Kernel normalization in float64 is the source of truth for this check.
Limitations
  • Only validates discrete kernel normalization, not broader optical energy accounting.
Artifacts
  • Log-scale wavelength vs normalization error plot
human: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓
dog: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓
cat: max |sum-1| = 4.44e-16 ✓

Snellen Acuity PASS

Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Snellen acuity prediction
Expected outcome
human < dog ≈ cat; within published ranges
Observed outcome
human=2.0′, dog=8.0′, cat=8.0′
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Each species falls within its target range and human acuity is best.
Inputs summary
Species-specific acuity prediction using Snellen E orientation discriminability.
Method
Predict minimum resolvable angular size for each species and compare with expected ranges and ordering.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/acuity.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/snellen.py
Assumptions
  • Published acuity ranges are broad PoC acceptance bands.
  • Letter-region masking and Pearson correlation distance are required.
Limitations
  • Cat predictions can be seed-sensitive near threshold.
Artifacts
  • Snellen stimulus examples
  • Predicted acuity bar chart
human: predicted 2.0 arcmin (expected 1.0–5.0) ✓
dog: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
cat: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
Ordering human ≤ dog ≤ cat: human=2.0, dog=8.0, cat=8.0 ✓

Dichromat Confusion PASS

Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Dichromat confusion axis
Expected outcome
Species-specific confusion medians are suppressed relative to controls, and human exceeds cat on the cat confusion set
Observed outcome
human medians: dog-conf=0.0493, cat-conf=0.1518, control=0.3397 dog medians: dog-conf=0.0295, cat-conf=0.1173, control=0.3594 cat medians: dog-conf=0.0463, cat-conf=0.0138, control=0.2345 Dog confusion median < dog control median: ✓ Cat confusion median < cat control median: ✓ Human > cat on cat confusion set: ✓ Dog control median > 0.05: ✓ Cat control median > 0.05: ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Each species-specific confusion median is suppressed relative to its controls, and human exceeds cat on the cat confusion set.
Inputs summary
Fixed confusion-pair panels for dog and cat plus a shared control-pair panel.
Method
Compute species-wise discriminability distributions across deterministic confusion/control stimulus matrices and compare confusion medians against controls.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/dichromat.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/ishihara.py
Assumptions
  • Stimulus scaling is applied to keep Naka-Rushton responses out of saturation.
Limitations
  • Compact panel intended for deterministic regression rather than exhaustive colour-space coverage.
Artifacts
  • Median discriminability bar chart
human medians: dog-conf=0.0493, cat-conf=0.1518, control=0.3397
dog medians: dog-conf=0.0295, cat-conf=0.1173, control=0.3594
cat medians: dog-conf=0.0463, cat-conf=0.0138, control=0.2345

Cat confusion median < cat control median: ✓

Nyquist Sampling PASS

Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limit
Expected outcome
Species Nyquist estimates fall within target cpd bands
Observed outcome
human: cone_density=200000/mm², spacing=2.40µm, nyquist=81.0 cpd (target 45-90) ✓ dog: cone_density=12000/mm², spacing=9.81µm, nyquist=15.1 cpd (target 10-30) ✓ cat: cone_density=10000/mm², spacing=10.75µm, nyquist=15.0 cpd (target 8-25) ✓
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Each species falls inside its target cycles/degree band.
Inputs summary
Area-centralis cone densities converted to spacing and cycles/degree for all species.
Method
Convert local cone density to spacing, then convert spacing to cycles/degree using focal-length-based retinal magnification.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
Assumptions
  • Hexagonal spacing approximation is used to map cone density to Nyquist limit.
Limitations
  • Uses density-model predictions at the patch centre rather than a full eccentricity sweep.
Artifacts
  • Species Nyquist-limit bar chart
human: cone_density=200000/mm², spacing=2.40µm, nyquist=81.0 cpd (target 45-90) ✓
dog: cone_density=12000/mm², spacing=9.81µm, nyquist=15.1 cpd (target 10-30) ✓
cat: cone_density=10000/mm², spacing=10.75µm, nyquist=15.0 cpd (target 8-25) ✓

Receptor Count PASS

Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Receptor count validation
Expected outcome
Measured counts and cone/rod ratios stay within density-model tolerance bands over the simulated patch
Observed outcome
human: measured=191176, expected=191233, count_err=0.000, cone_ratio_err=0.004 ✓ dog: measured=144374, expected=144474, count_err=0.001, cone_ratio_err=0.003 ✓ cat: measured=191710, expected=192048, count_err=0.002, cone_ratio_err=0.010 ✓
Tolerance
0.25
Pass criterion
Total count error < 25% and cone/rod composition error < 15% for every species.
Inputs summary
Generated mosaics compared against density-model expectations over the simulated square patch.
Method
Compare measured receptor counts and cone/rod composition against integrated density-model expectations over the simulated patch.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
Assumptions
  • Species density functions are the literature-backed PoC source of truth for expected counts.
Limitations
  • Current model still uses simplified analytic density functions rather than digitised histology maps.
Artifacts
  • Measured vs expected count bars
human: measured=191176, expected=191233, count_err=0.000, cone_ratio_err=0.004 ✓
dog: measured=144374, expected=144474, count_err=0.001, cone_ratio_err=0.003 ✓

Color Deficit Reproduction PASS

Stage
e2e
Architecture reference
Architecture §11d Known visual deficit reproduction
Expected outcome
Human sees R/G difference; dog does not
Observed outcome
human Δ=0.2705, dog Δ=0.1493
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Human left/right difference exceeds dog by at least 20%.
Inputs summary
Red-green split stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
Method
Measure left/right cone-response difference after full-pipeline simulation.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/pipeline.py
  • retinal_sim/output/voronoi.py
Assumptions
  • Left/right response norm is used as the summary metric.
Limitations
  • This is a compact proxy for a richer end-to-end color-deficit comparison.

  • Input stimulus

Resolution Gradient PASS

Stage
e2e
Architecture reference
Architecture §11d Resolution gradient
Expected outcome
Human center contrast exceeds human periphery and dichromat center contrast on a fine checkerboard
Observed outcome
human: center_contrast=0.0223, periphery_contrast=0.0199 dog: center_contrast=0.0171, periphery_contrast=0.0176 cat: center_contrast=0.0171, periphery_contrast=0.0174 Human center > human periphery: ✓ Human center > dog center: ✓ Human center > cat center: ✓
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Human center contrast exceeds human periphery and exceeds dog/cat center contrast.
Inputs summary
Checkerboard stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
Method
Compare center vs peripheral bright/dark response contrast on a fine checkerboard and compare human center contrast against dichromat center contrast.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/pipeline.py
  • retinal_sim/output/reconstruction.py
  • retinal_sim/output/voronoi.py
Assumptions
  • Checkerboard bright/dark contrast is used as the resolvability proxy.
Limitations
  • Quantifies contrast retention on a fine checkerboard but does not yet fit a full eccentricity-dependent MTF curve.
Artifacts
  • Input checkerboard
  • Per-species Voronoi images
  • Per-species reconstructions
